MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Yvonne McGovern, Esq. 702-333-0007 of Bergstrom Law for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Melissa Griffin, 415-225-2752 Defendant in Pro Se.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues counsel was not a member of the California bar therefore filing was a nullity and standing. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MELISSA GRIFFIN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Melissa Griffin's motion for summary judgment on the sole cause of action for breach of contract alleged against her in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Arrowood Indemnity Company is denied. Ms. Griffin failed to satisfy her summary judgment burden of showing that Arrowood lacks standing to bring the breach of contract claim against her. Ms. Griffin's Facts 6 and 7 state that Arrowhead asserts that it has standing to sue on the three loan contracts due to an assignment from The Student Loan Corporation. However, Ms. Griffin does not have any facts in her separate statement showing that: 1)Citibank did not assign its rights to the three loan contracts to the SLC or an assignor of the SLC or 2) the SLC did not have any rights in the three loan contracts that it could assign to Arrowood. While it is undisputed that the SLC is not a signatory to the loan contracts, that fact is insufficient to prove that the SLC did not at some point obtain the rights of the lender in the three loan contracts and/or did not have any rights in those loan contracts to assign to Arrowood.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Brenda Crow, CSR#10503, bcrow@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of The Issues
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Yvonne Q. McGovern of the Bergstrom Law Ltd., Tel. No. 1-702-232-1782 appearing
for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Melissa Griffin, Tel. No. 1-415-225-2752, Defendant appearing in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Arrowood Indemnity Company's motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue whether the parties settled the dispute and defendant Melissa Griffin fully performed the terms of the settlement. (Griffin Dec. pars. 21-25). Ms. Griffin's declaration states that she and Arrowood's agent (Regional Adjustment Bureau) agreed to settle the matter for $5,600 and she made all of the required payments. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hl

MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MELISSA GRIFFIN's Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff'S Counsel For Violating Rules Of Court And Local Rules.

Off calendar per request of the moving party.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Decaration Of Melissa Griffin
Off Calendar Mar-15-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Melissa Griffin, T:415.225.2752, defendant/moving party appearing on her own behalf;
Yvonne Q. Mcgovern, Esq., of Bergstrom Law, Ltd., T:702.232.1782, for plaintiff/opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MELISSA GRIFFIN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On The First Amended Complaint is denied. CCP 361, not governmental interest analysis, provides the rule for determining which state's statute of limitations applies to this case. (McCann v. Foster Wheeler, LLC (2010) 48 Cal. 4th 68, 87). For purposes of CCP 361, a cause of action on a claimed debt arises where the payments on the debt were to be made. (Western Coal & Mining Co. (1946) 27 Cal. 2d 819, 829). Although the place of payment may be the same as the residence of the payee, it is not always. Because the face of the complaint and judicially noticeable documents do not necessarily show that Ms. Griffin was required to make her loan payments to plaintiff Arrowhead Indemnity Company in North Carolina, this motion which is based on the application of North Carolina' statute of limitations, must be denied.

Counsel for Arrowood is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
